macro_segments.title: Resolution of Ma'amar after Ma'amar and Get after Get through the lens of Zika

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Daf: Ma'amar after Ma'amar and Get after Get
Rabban Gamliel's position: Ein Ma'amar Achar Ma'amar and how it applies to Chalitzah after Get
Gemara's question on Rabban Gamliel versus Shmuel: Does Yesh Zika or Ein Zika explain the difference?
Resolution: Both Rabban Gamliel and Rabbanan hold Ein Zika; Chalitzah after Get is valid without needing all co-wives

macro_segments.title: The Gezeirah (Rabbinic Decree) against Yibum after Ma'amar with two co-wives

micro_segments.title: Amar Mar: Rabban Gamliel's position on Ma'amar with two co-wives—ignore the second, do Get and Chalitzah with the first
Gemara's question: If the second Ma'amar is meaningless, why must he break off from the first via Get?
Gemara's answer: Gezeirah—fear people will confuse this case and permit Yibum after the second Ma'amar in the future

macro_segments.title: Survey of Tannaim who hold Ma'amar Koneh Kinyan Gamur (Complete Acquisition)

micro_segments.title: Rabban Gamliel: Ein Ma'amar Achar Ma'amar—second Ma'amar is meaningless
Beit Shammai: Ma'amar is like consummation; sister becomes Ervah and walks away
Rabbi Shimon: Bias Katan (intercourse of a minor) is like Ma'amar; Ein Bias Katan Achar Bias Katan
Ben Azzai: Ma'amar is meaningful in some cases but not others—debate between Rashi and Tosafot on the exact formulation
Rabbi Nechemiah: Any Bia or Chalitzah, even in the middle or after Get, ends the process—nothing counts afterward

macro_segments.title: Mishna: How is Ma'amar performed? Cash, Document, and Ketubah for Yibum

micro_segments.title: Gemara clarifies Mishna's Keitzad: It discusses hybrids (Ma'amar then Get, Get then Chalitzah) not Ma'amar after Ma'amar
Basic cases: Ma'amar then Get requires Chalitzah; Ma'amar then Yibum is proper procedure
Rav Huna: The Mitzvah of Yibum is to do Ma'amar first, then intercourse—paralleling normal Kiddushin before Nisu'in
Gemara's concern: If Ma'amar precedes intercourse, does it reframe Yibum as regular Kiddushin?
Answer: Even if it looks like regular Kiddushin, it is acceptable (Af Zu Kemitzvah); Rambam rules Ma'amar with Sheva Brachot

macro_segments.title: Rav's view: Lashes for intercourse without Ma'amar or improper Kiddushin

micro_segments.title: Gemara's initial question: Teaching of lashes for Bia without Ma'amar suggests Yibum doesn't work without Ma'amar
Resolution: Lashes are for violating rabbinic norms (Makat Mardut), not because the Yibum is invalid
Rav's broader principle: Lashes for Kiddushin via Bia without prior Shiduchim—values-based concern for proper relationship progression
Nahardai's view: Only lashes if both Kiddushin-via-Bia AND no Shiduchim combined; or even with Shiduchim if it's Pritzut (licentious)

macro_segments.title: Baraita on Ma'amar and Get of a Yevama: What words constitute a valid Ma'amar?

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Ma'amar is giving cash or Shtar; Rashi: Say words like Hitkadshi Li
Gemara: How do you write a Shtar for Yibum? The Ketubah obligations (Zun U'Farnesah) but lien on first husband's estate
Abiyi's question to Rabba: Does saying Harei At Megureshet and Mutteret to all men count as a valid Get of a Yevama?
Analysis: Get must look like a Get (Reiach HaGet); even a Pasul Get with smell of Get can make woman Gerusha for Kohen and affect Yibum

macro_segments.title: Rami bar Chama's questions on writing a Get for a future wife or sister-in-law

micro_segments.title: Case 1: Writing Get for Arusah (betrothed) before Nisu'in with intent to divorce after—Get is valid (Le'Arusah Damya)
Case 2: Writing Get for unrelated woman—not valid (Lo Arusah, Ain Get)
Rami bar Chama's inquiry: Can you write a Get for a Yevama before doing Ma'amar? Is she like an Arusah or not?
Two opinions: (1) She's connected via Zika so like Arusah; (2) No relationship yet so no Get—Teiku

macro_segments.title: Rav Chanina's question: Can a Get target only Zika or only Ma'amar, not both?

micro_segments.title: Scenario: After Ma'amar, Get intended only for Ma'amar, not Zika (or vice versa)—is it meaningful?
Initial analysis: Ma'amar is built on Zika; trying to sever only part is like divorcing half a woman—meaningless
Counterargument: Maybe Zika and Ma'amar are independent (Hai Lachud V'Hai Lachud) so attacking one works
Rava's case supports that Get can attack only Ma'amar (Natan Get Le'Maamaro Hutra Tzarata)
Conclusion: At least for Ma'amar part it works (Peshita L'Rava); whether for Zika part alone is unclear—Teiku

macro_segments.title: Chalitzah followed by Ma'amar: Does Ma'amar take effect after Chalitzah (when woman is Assur)?

micro_segments.title: Scenario: After Chalitzah to one wife, he does Ma'amar to her or the co-wife—is Ma'amar binding?
Issue: After Chalitzah she's Assur via Lav of Shuv Lo Yivneh; can Kiddushin (Ma'amar) attach to one in Issur Lav?
Rabbi Akiva's view: Ein Kiddushin Tosin B'Chayei Lavin—no Kiddushin binds when woman has Issur Lav; Ein Achar Chalitzah Klum
Chachamim disagree: Yesh Achar Chalitzah—even after Chalitzah, Ma'amar creates binding Kiddushin
Abiyi's resolution: Get of Yevama is only rabbinic, so Ma'amar works after Get but not after Chalitzah (which is de'Oraita Assur)

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's distinction: Ma'amar after Chalitzah depends on whether framed as regular Kiddushin or Yibum Kiddushin

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's view: If woman is told Hitkadshi Li B'Ma'amar Yevamin (as Yibum), it's not regular Kiddushin so doesn't take effect
Alternative: If framed as regular Kiddushin (Harei At Mekudeshet Li), it should bind; but Zika is broken by Chalitzah
Teaching of Baraita with Rabbi: After Chalitzah, Kiddushin L'Shum Ishus requires Get; L'Shum Yevamot does not
Chachamim disagree: Whether framed for Ishus or Yevamot, woman still needs Get; language of Harei At Mekudeshet Li binds
Rav Yosef's analogy: One plowing Ger's field not knowing which Ger—didn't intend Kinyan so no Kinyan; here you didn't intend normal Kiddushin intent
Abiyi's counter-analogy: Plowing field thinking it's Ger A but it's actually Ger B—still have Kinyan intent, so successful; similarly here, normal Kiddushin intent works
Resolution: Rabbi only doesn't hold if actual words framed it as Ma'amar Yevamin; Ma'amar builds on Zika which Chalitzah severed, so no Yevamin context remains

topical_tags.term: Shuv Lo Yivneh (she shall not be rebuilt)
